description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ayufan/openshift-cli Docker image and defines the stages build, test, deploy, review, staging, production, and cleanup. Add a production job in the production stage that runs the before_script commands `oc login \"$OPENSHIFT_SERVER\" --token=\"$OPENSHIFT_TOKEN\" --insecure-skip-tls-verify` and `oc project \"$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_PROJECT_ID\" 2> /dev/null || oc new-project \"$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_PROJECT_ID\"`, then executes the script commands `oc get services $APP 2> /dev/null || oc new-app . --name=$APP --strategy=docker`, `oc start-build $APP --from-dir=. --follow || sleep 3s && oc start-build $APP --from-dir=. --follow`, and `oc get routes $APP 2> /dev/null || oc expose service $APP --hostname=$APP_HOST`, with variables APP=production and APP_HOST=$CI_PROJECT_NAME.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN, runs only on the master branch and must be triggered manually. Add a review job in the review stage that uses the same before_script and script, sets APP=review-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME and APP_HOST=$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN, creates a review environment named review/$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME with an on_stop job stop-review, runs on all branch pipelines except master, and is not manual. Add a staging job in the staging stage that also uses the shared before_script and script, sets APP=staging and APP_HOST=$CI_PROJECT_NAME-staging.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN, runs only on the master branch, and deploys the staging application. Add a stop-review job in the cleanup stage that runs the before_script then executes `oc delete all -l \"app=$APP\"` with APP=review-$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, is manual, runs on branch pipelines except master, and sets GIT_STRATEGY=none; finally, add two test jobs (test1 and test2) in the test stage that have no before_script and simply run `echo run tests`.
